skill.name: shopify-development
skill.description: Build Shopify apps, extensions, themes using GraphQL Admin API, Shopify CLI, Polaris UI, and Liquid.
skill.summary: Complete Shopify development skill for building apps, extensions, and themes with validated GraphQL queries and OAuth authentication.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 553 potential issues, but evaluation confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES for legitimate Shopify development patterns. External commands are Shopify CLI tooling (shopify app/theme init, dev, deploy). Network calls target official Shopify domains (shopify.dev, myshopify.com). Environment access stores standard API credentials (SHOPIFY_API_KEY, SHOPIFY_API_SECRET). The skill follows Shopify best practices with OAuth CSRF protection and secure credential handling. Low risk due to legitimate development tooling usage.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution - Shopify CLI
low_findings.description: The skill executes Shopify CLI commands via subprocess. These are legitimate development tooling commands (shopify app init, shopify theme dev) but represent external code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shopify CLI commands are well-documented official tooling with hardcoded arguments, no user input injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Network Access - Shopify API Endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to Shopify domains (myshopify.com, shopify.dev) for OAuth authentication and GraphQL API operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls target official Shopify domains with HTTPS, proper OAuth flow, and CSRF state verification.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access - API Credentials
low_findings.description: The skill reads environment variables for Shopify API credentials (SHOPIFY_API_KEY, SHOPIFY_API_SECRET). This is standard practice but requires secure environment configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Credential access follows industry standards with .env file loading and no credential exfiltration patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build Shopify Apps, Extensions, and Themes
content.value_statement: Shopify development requires mastering multiple technologies: OAuth authentication, GraphQL APIs, Liquid templating, and Polaris UI components. This skill provides validated code patterns, CLI workflows, and API integration guides to accelerate your Shopify projects.
actual_capabilities: Initialize Shopify apps, extensions, and themes using Shopify CLI commands
Generate validated GraphQL queries and mutations for Admin API 2026-01
Implement OAuth 2.0 authentication flow with CSRF state verification
Create checkout UI extensions, admin actions, and POS customizations
Build Liquid theme templates with sections, snippets, and metafields
Configure webhooks, billing subscriptions, and Shopify Functions
limitations: Requires Shopify partner account and development store for testing
GraphQL queries validated against API 2026-01 may need updates for future versions
Cannot execute Shopify CLI commands directly - provides guidance only
App deployment requires manual review and approval by Shopify

use_cases.title: E-commerce Merchant Building Custom Tools
use_cases.description: Create apps to automate inventory management, generate custom reports, or integrate third-party services like shipping providers.

use_cases.title: Agency Developer Building Client Solutions
use_cases.description: Rapidly scaffold client projects with checkout customizations, admin extensions, and theme modifications using proven patterns.

use_cases.title: Indie Developer Launching Shopify Apps
use_cases.description: Build and monetize apps using billing integration, webhooks, and Shopify Functions for the Shopify App Store.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize a New Shopify App
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Shopify app with Node.js and React using the Shopify CLI. Include OAuth authentication, GraphQL API client setup, and basic product listing page. Configure access scopes for reading products and orders.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Checkout UI Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a checkout UI extension that adds a custom discount code field and gift message input. Use Polaris components and the checkout extension API. Include TypeScript types and example localization strings.

prompt_templates.title: Create GraphQL Query for Products
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a GraphQL query to fetch products with variants, images, and metafields. Include cursor-based pagination and filter by collection. Validate against Shopify Admin API 2026-01 schema.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Webhook Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a webhook handler for order creation events. Verify HMAC signature, parse the webhook payload, and trigger a fulfillment workflow. Include error handling and retry logic for failed deliveries.

output_examples.input: Create a product query with pagination

output: {% comment %} Product template with sections {% endcomment %}

best_practices: Always verify OAuth state parameter to prevent CSRF attacks during app installation
Use cursor-based pagination for GraphQL queries to handle large datasets efficiently
Store API credentials in environment variables, never commit secrets to version control
Implement webhook signature verification using HMAC-SHA256 for all webhook handlers
Follow Polaris design system for consistent UI across Shopify admin extensions
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API credentials or access tokens directly in source code files
Making unauthenticated GraphQL requests without proper OAuth token exchange
Ignoring rate limit headers (X-Shopify-Shop-Domain) and query cost in API responses
Using REST API when GraphQL provides more efficient batch operations
Skipping webhook HMAC verification which allows malicious webhook injection

faq.question: What Shopify API version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill uses Shopify Admin API 2026-01. GraphQL queries and mutations are validated against this version. Shopify releases new API versions quarterly with 12-month support windows.

faq.question: Do I need a Shopify Partner account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a free Shopify Partner account to create development stores, register apps, and access the Partner Dashboard for app submission and billing configuration.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Shopify Plus features?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers Shopify Plus features including checkout extensions, Shopify Functions for custom discounts and delivery rules, and B2B functionality on the Plus plan.

faq.question: How do I test my app before publishing to the App Store?
faq.answer: Use a development store from your Partner Dashboard to test apps locally with 'shopify app dev'. Test on multiple store types and use the Shopify App Review checklist before submission.

faq.question: What is the difference between app extensions and theme extensions?
faq.answer: App extensions add functionality to Shopify admin or checkout (backend logic). Theme extensions modify storefront appearance using Liquid (frontend UI). Many projects require both working together.

faq.question: How does billing work for Shopify apps?
faq.answer: Shopify handles billing through the Billing API. You can charge one-time fees, recurring subscriptions, or usage-based pricing. Shopify takes a revenue share and handles payment processing.
